picks.title: Jodeci
picks.blurb: R&B legends bring their classic hits to a packed venue. Nostalgic night of 90s soul.

picks.title: Bloodywood
picks.blurb: Metal pioneers merge Indian folk instruments with aggressive metal—a visceral, boundary-breaking set.

picks.title: Mystic Garden: Echo's of Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Electronic music festival celebrating Amsterdam artists across techno, house, trance on Sloterpark lakeside.
picks.why: New concept, 18+, sold out in presale
picks.source: Mystic Garden/Follow the Beat · DJ Guide / Mystic Garden Festival

picks.title: Danh Vo: πνεῦμα (Ἔλισσα)
picks.blurb: Major survey of Vietnamese-Austrian artist's spiritual, site-specific installations. Final weeks before August closure.

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.blurb: World Pride celebration: Eye's ode to queer filmmakers worldwide, opening this weekend during Amsterdam's Pride festivities.
picks.why: World Pride in Amsterdam, opens this weekend

picks.title: Beyond the Manosphere: Masculinities Today
picks.blurb: Timely exhibition exploring contemporary masculinity through art, film, and cultural commentary. Last weeks before August.

picks.title: Ed van der Elsken Photography Exhibition
picks.blurb: Opening Dutch photographer's 20th-century images at prestigious photography museum in historic canal houses.

picks.title: Celeste
picks.blurb: British indie/soul artist brings moody, introspective tracks to iconic Amsterdam venue.

picks.title: Nxt Museum: Pioneers of Media Art
picks.blurb: Immersive large-scale fine art installations exploring intersection of art, technology & innovation.

picks.title: Escape Club Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Late-night club with resident DJs on rotation. Sun 21 Jun closes earlier at 04:00.

picks.title: John Summit
picks.blurb: House and lofi beats in a courtyard setting. Perfect summer Sunday outdoor electronic music.

picks.title: Kho Liang Ie: Mid-Century Modernist
picks.blurb: Furniture and design from influential Dutch modernist. Clean lines, functional beauty—a family-friendly visual feast.
picks.why: Design icon; accessible to all ages

picks.title: Port Festival
picks.blurb: Ship tours, demonstrations, free boat rides, and all-ages activities on the waterfront.

picks.title: Sunday Clay Club
picks.blurb: Three-hour hands-on ceramics intro: hand-building and pottery wheel. Shape your own mug, bowl or small plate.

picks.title: 24 Hours Nieuw-West Festival (Free Workshops & Activities)
picks.blurb: Day-long free event: street art tours, brewery tastings, bird watching, DJ sets, workshops across the district.

picks.title: Rollende Keukens (Food Truck Festival)
picks.blurb: Street food market with live music, Beautiful Things Market stroll, Sound Festival. Mooie Dingen Markt & Klankenfestijn.
picks.why: Free entry; live music & market combo

picks.title: Bacchus Wijnfestival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's oldest wine festival returns. Three weekends of tastings, food pairings, and conviviality.
picks.why: Classic city wine tradition; final weekend of Bacchus

picks.title: Guns N' Roses and Mammoth
picks.blurb: Legendary rock act performs at Amsterdam's flagship arena. Major draw for the weekend.

picks.title: Amstelpark Food Festival (with Workshops)
picks.blurb: Food festival with free workshops: learn to fold dumplings, cocktail masterclass, live music, worldwide cuisine.
picks.source: I amsterdam / Your Little Black Book

picks.title: De Binnenstad - Echo's of Amsterdam Afterparty
picks.blurb: Hard house and trance afterparty for Mystic Garden festival with Auramatic, Jämo, Lacaty.

picks.title: Finale Koninklijk Concertgebouw Concours 2026
picks.blurb: Six mini-concerts showcase young classical musicians competing in this prestigious finals. Inspiring and accessible for young audiences.
picks.why: Final weekend of prestigious young musician competition

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Major food festival showcasing Amsterdam's culinary scene across three days with tastings, demos, and local vendors.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam / biteofamsterdam.com · Bite of Amsterdam / I amsterdam · I amsterdam / Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam · I amsterdam / Bite of Amsterdam

picks.title: Holland Festival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's leading international theatre and music festival closes this weekend with cutting-edge performances across multiple genres.
picks.source: Songkick · I amsterdam · Songkick / Holland Festival

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb: Sunday market featuring local artisans, handmade crafts, food stalls, and live music on Museumplein.
picks.why: Third Sunday of month, free entry

picks.title: Bacchus Wine Festival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's oldest wine festival combines outdoor tastings with food stalls and live music in the leafy Bos park.
picks.why: Three-week festival, this is final weekend
picks.source: I amsterdam · I amsterdam / Amsterdam Experience · Amsterdam Tips / Bart's Boekje

picks.title: Open Garden Days
picks.blurb: Visit 25+ private canal gardens hidden behind historic houses. Special boat transport between locations. Unique access to rarely-seen green spaces.
picks.why: Only weekend it's open all year
picks.source: I amsterdam · I amsterdam / Guide Amsterdam

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: GRIMM (8+) — Hiphop-Ballet Fairytales
picks.blurb: Rethink classic fairy tales through explosive hip-hop and ballet fusion. Darker, funnier, spectacularly choreographed for families.

picks.title: 24 Hours Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: District-wide day of free activities: museum parties, street art tours, brewery tastings, DJ sets, bird-watching, workshops.
picks.why: Large free community event across entire neighborhood

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: Havenfestival 2026 — Java Island
picks.blurb: Free harbour activities, demonstrations, and hands-on fun for all ages across Java Island's waterfront.

picks.title: LuminAir Father's Day Special
picks.blurb: Rooftop bar next to Central Station offering free whisky/cocktail for dads; panoramic city views.
picks.why: Father's Day celebration with skyline views

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen: Eye's curated classics programme, screened in 35mm whenever possible.
picks.why: Celluloid cinema at Amsterdam's flagship film museum

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day
